description: US utility assistance and shutoff protection: LIHEAP, state moratoria, temperature triggers, payment plans, arrearage programs. Federal baseline — state rules vary significantly.
source: LIHEAP statute 42 U.S.C. § 8621 et seq., HHS ACF LIHEAP guidance, NCLC utility shutoff resources, verified 2026

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program — federal block grant to states for home energy costs

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: 42 U.S.C. § 8621 et seq. (Energy Assistance Act, Title XXVI of Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act 1981)

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: heating/cooling cost assistance, crisis intervention grants, and weatherization for low-income households

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: at or below 150% federal poverty level (FPL), or 60% state median income — whichever is higher

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: households with elderly (60+), disabled members, or young children under 6 receive priority

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: state/local LIHEAP grantee — find via HHS ACF at acf.hhs.gov/ocs/map/liheap-map or 1-800-552-4867

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: funding is finite and seasonal; many states open enrollment in fall and close when funds are exhausted

triples.subject: LIHEAP crisis component
triples.object: emergency assistance for imminent shutoff, broken heating equipment, or unsafe conditions

triples.subject: LIHEAP crisis component
triples.object: must be delivered within 18 hours for life-threatening situations, 48 hours otherwise

triples.subject: utility shutoff moratorium
triples.object: temporary prohibition on disconnecting service, typically triggered by extreme temperatures

triples.subject: temperature-triggered shutoff ban
triples.object: many states ban shutoffs when outdoor temp is forecast below 32°F or above 95–100°F

triples.subject: temperature-triggered shutoff ban
triples.object: California bans shutoffs during heat advisories; Minnesota bans Oct 15–Apr 15; Texas has limited protections

triples.subject: utility shutoff
triples.object: no federal law prevents shutoffs for inability to pay, but states and utilities have their own rules

triples.subject: medical baseline rate
triples.object: reduced utility rate for households with qualifying medical conditions (e.g., life-support equipment)

triples.subject: medical baseline rate
triples.object: California (CARE/FERA programs), New York, and other states — check state PUC or utility provider

triples.subject: shutoff notice requirement
triples.object: most states require 10–30 days written notice before disconnection; some require phone call first

triples.subject: payment arrangement
triples.object: most state utility commissions require utilities to offer payment plans before shutoff

triples.subject: payment arrangement
triples.object: ask for budget billing (level pay), arrearage management programs, and extended repayment schedules

triples.subject: arrearage management program
triples.object: utility program that forgives past-due balance incrementally when current bills are paid on time

triples.subject: arrearage management program
triples.object: PPL Electric (PA) forgives $5 per on-time payment; Consolidated Edison (NY) similar programs

triples.subject: CARE program California
triples.object: 30–35% discount on electricity and gas bills for income-qualified households

triples.subject: CARE program California
triples.object: household income at or below 200% FPL, or enrolled in qualifying public assistance program

triples.subject: FERA program California
triples.object: 18% discount on electricity for households with 3+ people at 200–250% FPL

triples.subject: Home Energy Assistance Target Program
triples.object: New York — HEAP provides heating/cooling assistance plus Emergency HEAP for crisis situations

triples.subject: utility reconnection fee
triples.object: many state commissions allow waiver of reconnection fees for low-income or medical-necessity households

triples.subject: third-party notification
triples.object: right to designate a third party (relative, social worker) to receive shutoff notices on your behalf

triples.subject: third-party notification
triples.object: most states — request from your utility company directly

triples.subject: Equal Pay or Budget Billing
triples.object: utility averages annual bill into equal monthly payments to prevent seasonal spikes

triples.subject: state utility commission
triples.object: file complaints about improper shutoffs, denied payment plans, or discrimination with state PUC or PSC

triples.subject: state utility commission
triples.object: search '[state] Public Utilities Commission' or '[state] Public Service Commission' for local contact
